title: Effervescent British Spinach Welsh Rarebit
description: A silky, grassy quick-fix Welsh rarebit featuring blanched spinach and a soda water‑light cheese sauce, perfect for a lacto‑vegetarian quick lunch.
instructions: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and blanch the spinach for 30 seconds, then transfer to ice water to stop cooking.
Drain spinach and squeeze out excess moisture, then chop coarsely.
Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat, add flour and stir constantly for 1 minute to make a roux.
Gradually whisk in soda water and milk, cooking until the mixture thickens, about 3 minutes.
Stir in grated cheddar, mustard, paprika, salt, and pepper until cheese melts into a smooth sauce.
Combine the blanched spinach into the sauce, stirring to coat evenly.
Spread the spinach mixture onto slices of toasted sourdough or rye bread.
Broil or grill until the top is golden and bubbly, about 2 minutes.
Serve immediately with a side of cucumber salad.
